scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Throughput gap clearly identified — 300/min inbound vs 15-20/min processing, with detailed math table showing 280/min deficit. (2) Queue growth consequence described — unbounded backlog, 16,800 messages after 1 hour, 14 hours to drain. (3) Visibility timeout double-processing risk identified — batch of 10 at 2-4s each = 20-40s exceeding 30s timeout, with specific remediation options. (4) Missing DLQ identified as a blocker with maxReceiveCount recommendation. (5) Queue depth metric gap identified as 'CRITICAL' and 'single most important metric.' (6) Autoscaling recommended as must-have with 15-20 worker instances minimum. (7) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional throughput analysis — not just identifying the gap but calculating deficit rate, 1-hour backlog, and drain time, plus framing it as '~6% of inbound traffic'
Visibility timeout analysis goes beyond the rubric — calculates that batch processing systematically exceeds timeout and offers three concrete remediation options
The '3am nightmare scenario' narrative in debugging_capability is outstanding operational thinking — healthy metrics while system is broken
Poison message analysis chains consequences: fail → return to visibility → fail again → consume worker capacity permanently
Escalations are well-routed — architecture decision to MJ, domain impact of duplicates to Bird
Identifies additional risks beyond rubric: missing circuit breakers, no graceful degradation, no backpressure signaling, rollback idempotency concerns
Recommendations are prioritized into blocking vs next-iteration, with specific implementation guidance
